Supplier.title: Supplier
Supplier.description: Represents a business entity that provides products to the company.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

name.description: The full name of the supplier.

email.description: The contact email address for the supplier.

phone.description: The contact phone number for the supplier.

country.description: The country where the supplier is located.

productsOffered.description: A list of product categories or names that the supplier offers.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the supplier record was created.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents an individual or business that purchases products from the company.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

name.description: The full name of the customer.

email.description: The contact email address for the customer.

phone.description: The contact phone number for the customer.

interestedProducts.description: A list of product categories or names the customer has expressed interest in.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the customer record was created.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a distinct item or service that the company manages and sells.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sku.description: The Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for the product, a unique identifier for inventory management.

category.description: The category the product belongs to (e.g., 'Electronics', 'Apparel').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product record was created.

Stock.title: Stock
Stock.description: Represents an entry of a product's stock, linked to a specific supplier.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Stock entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product associated with this stock entry. (Relationship: Product 1:N Stock)

supplierId.description: Reference to the Supplier providing this stock. (Relationship: Supplier 1:N Stock)

price.description: The price of the product from this supplier.

quantity.description: The available quantity of the product from this supplier.

leadTime.description: The estimated lead time (in days) for receiving this stock from the supplier.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this stock entry was created.

BestOffer.title: BestOffer
BestOffer.description: Represents the best available offer for a specific product, identified by price and lead time from a supplier.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BestOffer entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product for which this is the best offer. (Relationship: Product 1:N BestOffer)

bestPrice.description: The lowest price found for the associated product.

supplierId.description: Reference to the Supplier offering the best price. (Relationship: Supplier 1:N BestOffer)

leadTime.description: The lead time (in days) associated with this best offer from the specific supplier.

calculatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this best offer was last calculated or updated.

CustomerResponse.title: CustomerResponse
CustomerResponse.description: Records a customer's response to an offer, indicating their interest or action.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomerResponse entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who provided the response. (Relationship: Customer 1:N CustomerResponse)

bestOfferId.description: Reference to the BestOffer to which the customer responded. (Relationship: BestOffer 1:N CustomerResponse)

responseType.description: The type of response from the customer (e.g., 'order', 'quote', 'interest').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the customer's response was recorded.

AutomationLog.title: AutomationLog
AutomationLog.description: Logs events and status of automated business processes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AutomationLog entity.

pipelineName.description: The name of the automation pipeline that generated this log entry.

event.description: A brief description of the event that occurred in the automation.

status.description: The execution status of the automation event (e.g., 'success', 'failed', 'pending').

details.description: Detailed information or error messages related to the automation event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the automation event occurred.

definition.description: Stores core user profile information. Documents are owned by the authenticated user (`userId` matches `request.auth.uid`). Not part of initial schema, inferred from DBAC and auth requirements.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: A collection used for database-driven access control. The existence of a document at `/app_roles_admin/{userId}` grants the corresponding user administrative privileges across the application. Access is restricted to trusted processes or existing admins for management. Not part of initial schema, inferred from DBAC requirements.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of an administrator.

definition.description: Stores details for business suppliers. Accessible by authorized application users (e.g., admins).

params.name: supplierId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific supplier.

definition.description: Stores information for business customers. Accessible by authorized application users (e.g., admins).

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific customer.

definition.description: Stores details of products managed by the business. Accessible by authorized application users (e.g., admins).

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product.

definition.description: Manages inventory stock entries, linking products to specific suppliers. Accessible by authorized application users (e.g., admins).

params.name: stockId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific stock entry.

definition.description: Aggregates and stores the best available offer for a product from a supplier. Accessible by authorized application users (e.g., admins).

params.name: bestOfferId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific best offer.

definition.description: Records customer responses to offers. Accessible by authorized application users (e.g., admins).

params.name: customerResponseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific customer response.

definition.description: Logs events and status of automated business processes, primarily for monitoring and debugging by authorized users (e.g., admins).

params.name: automationLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific automation log entry.

definition.description: A collection whose documents are written by authorized dashboard users to signal external automation systems (e.g., n8n) to trigger best offer recalculations. Primarily write-only for users, read by backend systems. Not part of initial schema, inferred from application requirements.

params.name: triggerId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific offer recalculation trigger.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability in line with the core design principles. The primary authorization model for this Business Management Dashboard is role-based, where access to all core business entities (Suppliers, Customers, Products, Stocks, BestOffers, CustomerResponses, AutomationLogs) is determined by the authenticated user's assigned role within the application.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** Authorization independence is achieved by structuring collections such that access rules do not rely on `get()` operations to fetch data from parent documents or other collections to determine permissions. For all primary business entities (e.g., `/suppliers`, `/customers`), access is determined by checking the authenticated user's `uid` against a dedicated role collection, specifically `/app_roles_admin/{adminId}`. The mere existence of a document at this path for a given `userId` grants administrative privileges. This design completely eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies for the main business data. References between entities (e.g., `productId` in `stocks`) are for data relationships and querying, not for real-time authorization checks in security rules, thus ensuring atomic operations and ease of debugging.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure inherently supports Query-As-Permissions (QAPs) through **Structural Segregation** and **Role-Based Access Control**. Each collection (e0.g., `/products`, `/automationLogs`) is designed to house documents that share a homogeneous security posture. For instance, all documents in the `/suppliers` collection are considered 'managed by an authorized user'. Security rules will grant `list` and `read` access to these collections only if the `request.auth.uid` corresponds to an authorized role (e.g., a member of the `/app_roles_admin` collection). This means that any authorized user can retrieve *all* documents in a collection, eliminating the need for client-side filtering or complex server-side rule filtering, thus enabling efficient and secure `list` queries. The `offerRecalculationTriggers` collection is a specific example, designed for write-only access by authorized users to trigger backend processes, maintaining a clear security boundary for its purpose.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** User roles are stored explicitly in the database within the `/app_roles_admin/{adminId}` collection, leveraging the 'existence over content' principle. This means `request.auth.uid` is the sole determinant from the authentication layer, with all role information residing in Firestore. User profiles themselves are stored under `/users/{userId}` to maintain a clean separation of concerns.

**Consistency and Predictability:** Semantic naming conventions are used for all paths and wildcards (e.g., `{supplierId}`). Explicit references (like `productId`, `supplierId`, `customerId`) are included within documents where necessary for efficient data retrieval and display, without implying authorization dependencies. All timestamps are consistently named (e.g., `createdAt`, `timestamp`).
